sum1to10.title: Sum from 1 to 10
sum1to10.prompt: Compute the sum of the integers from 1 to 10.
sum1to10.explanation: `⍳10` spits out `1 2 … 10`. `+/` slips a `+` between each pair and folds the whole thing down to a number. Gauss's sum.

product1to5.title: Product from 1 to 5
product1to5.prompt: Compute the product of the integers from 1 to 5 (in other words, 5 factorial).
product1to5.explanation: Same machinery as `+/`, only with multiplication: `1×2×3×4×5`. That is `5!`.

vectorMin.title: Minimum of a vector
vectorMin.explanation: `⌊` between two numbers returns the smaller. `⌊/` runs it across the vector until a single value is left: the minimum.

vectorMax.title: Maximum of a vector
vectorMax.explanation: Mirror of `⌊/`, using `⌈` which keeps the larger of any two numbers.

average.title: Arithmetic mean
average.explanation: `+/` gives the sum `30`, `⍴` on a vector returns the length `5`. Divide and you have the mean.

length.title: Length of a vector
length.explanation: For a plain vector, shape and length are the same thing. `⍴` tells you straight away.

firstNEvens.title: First even numbers
firstNEvens.prompt: Generate the first ten positive even integers.
firstNEvens.explanation: `⍳10` produces `1..10`. Multiply every element by 2 and you get the even sequence.

firstNOdds.title: First odd numbers
firstNOdds.prompt: Generate the first ten positive odd integers.
firstNOdds.explanation: Build the evens as above, then subtract 1 from each. Watch out: `¯1` is the negative constant, not the operator `-` applied to `1`.

squares.title: Squares
squares.prompt: Compute the squares of the integers from 1 to 10.
squares.explanation: Recurring trap: in APL `*` is power, not multiplication. `(⍳10)*2` squares every element.

cubes.title: Cubes
cubes.prompt: Compute the cubes of the integers from 1 to 5.

reverseVector.title: Reverse a vector
reverseVector.explanation: Monadic `⌽` flips a vector along the last axis.

sortAscending.title: Sort ascending
sortAscending.explanation:
Key insight: `⍋` does not sort the vector. It returns the **recipe** of positions to follow in order to read it sorted.

On `3 1 4 1 5 9 2 6` the smallest value (the first `1`) sits at position 2. The next smallest (the other `1`) sits at position 4. Then `2` at position 7, `3` at position 1, and so on. The resulting recipe is `2 4 7 1 3 5 8 6`.

Then `⍵[...]` executes the recipe: it picks from `⍵` at those indices, in order. The sorted vector falls out.

The reason APL splits the two steps: the same recipe `⍋⍵` can sort a second, parallel vector (for example names ordered by age), which would be impossible if `⍋` consumed the indices on its own.

sortDescending.title: Sort descending
sortDescending.explanation: Same idea as `⍋`, but `⍒` produces the recipe for reading in descending order.

catenateVectors.title: Catenate two vectors
catenateVectors.prompt: Join 1 2 3 with 4 5 into a single vector.
catenateVectors.explanation: The comma glues two vectors along the last axis.

firstElement.title: First element
firstElement.explanation: Monadic `⊃` (first / disclose) pulls out the first element.

lastElement.title: Last element
lastElement.explanation: Reverse with `⌽`, then take the first. A classic trick for grabbing the last element without knowing the length.

dropFirst.title: Drop the first element
dropFirst.explanation: `↓` with a positive count throws that many elements off the front.

dropLast.title: Drop the last element
dropLast.explanation: `↓` with a negative count drops from the tail.

takeFirst3.title: Take the first three
takeFirst3.prompt: Take the first three elements of 10 20 30 40 50.
takeFirst3.explanation: `↑` with a positive count grabs the first N. Opposite of `↓`.

takeLast3.title: Take the last three
takeLast3.prompt: Take the last three elements of 10 20 30 40 50.
takeLast3.explanation: `↑` with a negative count grabs from the tail.

evenMask.title: Boolean mask of even values
evenMask.prompt: Build a 0/1 mask marking the even values among 1..10.
evenMask.explanation: `2|⍳10` is the remainder mod 2 for each element. Comparing it with `0` puts a `1` exactly on the even ones.

countEvens.title: Count the even numbers
countEvens.prompt: How many even integers are there between 1 and 50?
countEvens.explanation: The same even-mask, now over `1..50`. `+/` sums the `1`s of the mask: one per even number.

sumEvens.title: Sum the even numbers
sumEvens.prompt: Sum the even integers between 1 and 100.
sumEvens.explanation: Even-mask of `1..100`, then `/` uses it to keep only the elements where the mask holds `1` (the evens themselves). `+/` closes the sum.

squareRoot.title: Square root
squareRoot.explanation: Raising a number to `0.5` is the square root.

hypotenuse.title: Pythagorean hypotenuse
hypotenuse.prompt: Compute the hypotenuse of a right triangle with legs 3 and 4.
hypotenuse.explanation: `3² + 4² = 25`, and the square root of `25` is `5`. Pythagoras in one line.

absoluteValues.title: Absolute values
absoluteValues.prompt: Compute the absolute value of every element of ¯3 ¯1 0 2 4.
absoluteValues.explanation: Monadic `|` is the magnitude, the distance from zero.

negateVector.title: Negate a vector
negateVector.explanation: Monadic `-` flips the sign of every element.

rollDie.title: Roll a die
rollDie.explanation: Monadic `?` draws a random number between `1` and its argument.

rollFiveDice.title: Roll five dice
rollFiveDice.explanation: `5⍴6` builds the vector `6 6 6 6 6`. `?` over a vector rolls one die per element.

piAndE.title: π and e
piAndE.prompt: Compute π and the base of the natural logarithm e.
piAndE.explanation: Monadic `○` multiplies its argument by π, so `○1` is π. `*1` is `e¹ = e`. The comma sets them side by side.

fToC.title: Fahrenheit to Celsius
fToC.prompt: Convert 32, 100 and 212 from Fahrenheit to Celsius.
fToC.explanation: APL reads right to left. First `32 100 212 - 32` produces `0 68 180`, then everything is multiplied by `5÷9`.

cToF.title: Celsius to Fahrenheit
cToF.prompt: Convert 0, 37 and 100 from Celsius to Fahrenheit.
cToF.explanation: Multiply by `9÷5` (right to left, as always) and add `32`.

signOfEach.title: Sign of each value
signOfEach.prompt: Compute the sign of every element of ¯3 0 4 ¯7.
signOfEach.explanation: Monadic `×` is the signum: `¯1`, `0` or `1` depending on the sign.

ceilingFloor.title: Ceiling and floor
ceilingFloor.prompt: Compute the ceiling and the floor of 3.7.
ceilingFloor.explanation: `⌈` rounds up to the nearest integer, `⌊` rounds down. The comma puts them next to each other.

sumOfSquares.title: Sum of squares
sumOfSquares.prompt: Compute the sum of the squares of 1..10.

indicesOfOnes.title: Positions of the ones
indicesOfOnes.prompt: List the positions of the 1s in 0 1 0 1 1 0.
indicesOfOnes.explanation: `⍸` returns the positions where a boolean vector holds `1`.

membershipTest.title: Membership test
membershipTest.explanation: `∊` walks the left vector and marks `1` whenever the element shows up on the right, `0` otherwise.

positionOfItem.title: Position of an item
positionOfItem.explanation: Dyadic `⍳` looks for the right-hand value inside the left vector and returns the first index where it appears.

cumulativeSum.title: Running totals
cumulativeSum.explanation: `\` is the version of `/` that keeps the intermediate values. `+\` produces `1`, `1+2`, `1+2+3`, and so on.

cumulativeProduct.title: Running products
cumulativeProduct.explanation: Running totals with `×`. Each step is a partial factorial.

runningMax.title: Running maximum
runningMax.explanation: At every position, `⌈\` keeps the largest value seen so far.

argmax.title: Index of the maximum
argmax.prompt: Find the index of the largest element in 3 1 4 1 5 9 2 6.
argmax.explanation: `⍒` is the descending recipe; its first entry is the position of the maximum. The `9` sits in the sixth slot.

argmin.title: Index of the minimum
argmin.prompt: Find the index of the smallest element in 3 1 4 1 5 9 2 6.
argmin.explanation: Mirror of argmax: `⍋` is the ascending recipe and its first entry is the position of the minimum.

topThree.title: Three largest values
topThree.prompt: Return the three largest values of 5 1 9 3 7 2 8.
topThree.explanation: `{⍵[⍒⍵]}` sorts descending, producing `9 8 7 5 3 2 1`. Then `3↑` grabs the first three.

distinctValues.title: Distinct values
distinctValues.explanation: Monadic `∪` keeps one copy of every value, in first-seen order.

countDistinct.title: Count distinct values
countDistinct.prompt: How many distinct values are there in 1 2 2 3 1 4 4?
countDistinct.explanation: Take the unique values and read off the length with `⍴`.

unionVectors.title: Union of two vectors
unionVectors.explanation: Dyadic `∪` fuses the two vectors and drops duplicates.

intersectionVectors.title: Intersection of two vectors
intersectionVectors.explanation: `∩` keeps only the elements present in both vectors.

bitmaskToPmOne.title: Booleans to ±1
bitmaskToPmOne.prompt: Map the booleans 0 1 0 1 1 to the values ¯1 1 ¯1 1 1.
bitmaskToPmOne.explanation: Common trick: double the bit and shift down by one. `0` becomes `¯1+0 = ¯1`, `1` becomes `¯1+2 = 1`. Useful when a boolean mask has to turn into signs.

clampZeroOne.title: Clamp to [0, 1]
clampZeroOne.prompt: Clamp the values ¯1 0.5 2 to the interval between 0 and 1.
clampZeroOne.explanation: Right to left, as always. `1 ⌊ ¯1 0.5 2` imposes a ceiling of `1`, yielding `¯1 0.5 1`. Then `0 ⌈ …` raises the floor to `0`. Clamp in two strokes.

replaceNegWithZero.title: Zero out the negatives
replaceNegWithZero.prompt: Replace any negative value of ¯3 ¯1 0 2 4 with zero.
replaceNegWithZero.explanation: `0 ⌈ v` keeps the larger of `0` and each element. Negatives get lifted to `0`.

reshape3x4.title: Reshape into a 3×4 matrix
reshape3x4.explanation: Dyadic `⍴` fills the requested shape with the right-hand vector, row by row.

rowSums.title: Row sums
rowSums.prompt: Sum each row of a 3×4 matrix.
rowSums.explanation: `+/` over a matrix reduces along the last axis: one sum per row.

columnSums.title: Column sums
columnSums.prompt: Sum each column of a 3×4 matrix.
columnSums.explanation: Same idea as `+/` but with `⌿` (slash with bar), which acts on the first axis: one sum per column.

matrixTranspose.title: Matrix transpose
matrixTranspose.prompt: Transpose a 2×3 matrix into a 3×2 matrix.
matrixTranspose.explanation: Monadic `⍉` swaps rows and columns. A 2×3 becomes a 3×2.

identityMatrix.title: Identity pattern
identityMatrix.prompt: Build a 5×5 matrix with 1 on the diagonal and 0 elsewhere.
identityMatrix.explanation: `∘.f` builds a table by comparing every pair `(i,j)`. With `=`, the cell is `1` only where `i=j`: the diagonal.

multiplicationTable.title: Multiplication table
multiplicationTable.explanation: Outer product `∘.×`: every `i` from the left is paired with every `j` from the right and the two are multiplied. Whole table in one line.

outerSum.title: Outer sum
outerSum.prompt: Build the outer sum of 1..3 with itself.
outerSum.explanation: Outer product with `+`: every cell is `i+j`.

dotProduct.title: Dot product
dotProduct.prompt: Compute the dot product of 1 2 3 and 4 5 6.
dotProduct.explanation: `+.×` multiplies element-wise (`4 10 18`) and then sums. The classical inner product.

matrixMultiply.title: Matrix multiplication
matrixMultiply.prompt: Multiply a 2×3 matrix by a 3×2 matrix.
matrixMultiply.explanation: Over matrices, `+.×` is the standard matrix product: row by column.

binaryToInt.title: Binary to integer
binaryToInt.prompt: Convert the binary digits 1 0 1 1 to a decimal integer.
binaryToInt.explanation: `⊥` (decode) reads the right vector as digits in the base given on the left. `1011` in base 2 is `11`.

intToBinary8.title: Integer to 8-bit binary
intToBinary8.prompt: Encode the number 11 as eight binary digits.
intToBinary8.explanation: `⊤` (encode) is the inverse of `⊥`. `8⍴2` asks for eight digits, all in base 2.

hammingWeight.title: Hamming weight
hammingWeight.prompt: Count how many bits are set in the 8-bit representation of 11.
hammingWeight.explanation: Encode `11` in 8 bits, then `+/` sums the digits. That is the popcount.

filterPositives.title: Keep only positive values
filterPositives.prompt: Keep only the strictly positive values of ¯2 ¯1 0 1 2.
filterPositives.explanation: `0<v` produces the mask `0 0 0 1 1`. `/` uses it to keep only the elements where the mask is `1`.

pascalRow.title: Pascal's triangle row
pascalRow.explanation: Dyadic `!` is the binomial coefficient `(k choose 6)`. With `k` running from `0` to `6` (that is `¯1+⍳7`), you obtain the entire row.

factorialReduce.title: Factorial via reduce
factorialReduce.prompt: Compute 7! using the reduce operator on multiplication.

palindromeNumeric.title: Is it a palindrome?
palindromeNumeric.prompt: Check whether 1 2 3 2 1 reads the same forwards and backwards.
palindromeNumeric.explanation: Compare the vector with its reverse: you get `1` everywhere if it is a palindrome. `∧/` (AND of all bits) is `1` only when every position matches.

countA.title: Count letters
countA.prompt: How many times does the letter 'a' appear in 'banana'?
countA.explanation: `'banana'='a'` compares character by character, producing `0 1 0 1 0 1`. `+/` sums.

countVowels.title: Count vowels
countVowels.prompt: How many vowels are there in 'computation'?
countVowels.explanation: `∊` against `'aeiou'` marks `1` on the letters that are vowels. The sum counts them: `o, u, a, i, o`.

findSubstring.title: Find a substring
findSubstring.prompt: Find every position where 'ana' appears inside 'banana'.
findSubstring.explanation: `⍷` places a `1` at every position where the searched string starts inside the larger one. `⍸` collects those indices.

keepVowels.title: Filter the vowels
keepVowels.explanation: Same vowel-mask as the count, but used as compression (`/`) against the string: only the vowels survive.

vectorMean.title: Mean of a vector
vectorMean.explanation: Sum `108` divided by length `6` gives `18`. Same pattern as `average`, just with a longer vector.

variance.title: Variance
variance.explanation: Mean: `40÷8 = 5`. Deviations from the mean: `¯3 ¯1 ¯1 ¯1 0 0 2 4`. Squared they sum to `32`. Divided by `8` you get `4`. Population variance.

stddev.title: Standard deviation
stddev.explanation: Square root of the previous variance: `√4 = 2`.

composeWithDfn.title: Define an anonymous function
composeWithDfn.prompt: Define an inline function that squares its argument, and apply it to 7.
composeWithDfn.explanation: Braces `{…}` define an anonymous function (a dfn). Inside, `⍵` is the right argument.

defineAndReuse.title: Name and reuse a function
defineAndReuse.prompt: Bind a name sq to the squaring function and apply it to 1..5.
defineAndReuse.explanation: `←` binds a name to a function exactly the way you would do with a variable. `⋄` separates statements on the same line.

eachOperator.title: Each operator
eachOperator.prompt: Apply the squaring function to every element of 1 2 3 4.
eachOperator.explanation: `¨` applies the function one element at a time. Redundant here (`*2` already works on the whole vector), but it shows the pattern.

commute.title: Swap the arguments
commute.explanation: `f⍨` swaps the arguments before applying `f`. So `10 -⍨ 3` runs `3 - 10`.

selfApply.title: Self-apply
selfApply.prompt: Compute 5+5 by using each side as the same number.
selfApply.explanation: Used monadically, `f⍨ ⍵` is equivalent to `⍵ f ⍵`. Hence `+⍨ 5` is `5+5`.

manhattan.title: Two-argument function
manhattan.prompt: Define manhattan as the Manhattan distance between two vectors, then call it on 1 2 3 and 4 6 8.
manhattan.explanation: Inside a dyadic dfn `⍺` is the left argument and `⍵` the right. Element-wise differences `¯3 ¯4 ¯5`, absolute values `3 4 5`, sum `12`. The taxicab distance.

sumOfSquaresOneLine.title: Sum of squares in one line
sumOfSquaresOneLine.prompt: Compute the sum of the squares of 1..5 using each and reduce together.

indicesOfEvens.title: Indices of even values
indicesOfEvens.prompt: List the positions of the even values in 4 7 8 11 12.
indicesOfEvens.explanation: `2|v` gives `0 1 0 1 0`. `0=` marks the evens with `1`, yielding `1 0 1 0 1`. `⍸` returns the positions.

replicateByVector.title: Replicate by counts
replicateByVector.explanation: Dyadic `/` replicates every right-hand item as many times as the matching left-hand count says.

expandZeros.title: Expand with zeros
expandZeros.prompt: Insert a zero between every pair of values in 7 8 9.
expandZeros.explanation: `\` uses the mask `1 0 1 0 1` as a stencil: on a `1` it places the next source value, on a `0` it inserts a fill (zero for numbers).

rotateVector.title: Rotate a vector
rotateVector.explanation: Dyadic `⌽` rotates. A positive count on the left means a leftward rotation by that many slots.

medianOdd.title: Median (odd length)
medianOdd.explanation: Sort, compute the middle position `⌈5÷2 = 3`, pick that element. Works only for odd lengths.

countOccurrences.title: Count occurrences
countOccurrences.prompt: How many times does 2 appear inside 1 2 3 2 4 2?
countOccurrences.explanation: `2=v` produces the mask of twos. Summing the mask counts them.

isSortedAscending.title: Is the vector sorted?
isSortedAscending.prompt: Check whether 1 2 3 5 4 is sorted in ascending order.
isSortedAscending.explanation: `1↓v` drops the first element, `¯1↓v` drops the last. Comparing them position-wise with `≥` gives the consecutive pairs: `1 1 1 0`. `∧/` is `0` because `4` is not `≥ 5`.

primesUpTo20.title: Primes up to 20
primesUpTo20.prompt: List every prime number up to 20.
primesUpTo20.explanation:
Core idea: a number is prime when it has exactly two divisors (1 and itself).

`(⍳20)∘.|⍳20` builds a table where cell `(i,j)` equals `i mod j`. `0=` turns that into `1` whenever the division is exact. `+⌿` sums per column, giving the divisor count of each candidate. `2=` isolates the ones with exactly two divisors. `/` filters `⍳20` through that mask.

sumOfDigits.title: Sum of decimal digits
sumOfDigits.prompt: Compute the sum of the decimal digits of 12345.
sumOfDigits.explanation: `⍕12345` formats the number into the character vector `'12345'`. Dyadic `⍳` against `'0123456789'` looks up each character among the digits, returning 1-based positions `2 3 4 5 6`. `¯1+` shifts them back into `0..9`, giving `1 2 3 4 5`. `+/` sums to `15`.

stringLength.title: String length
stringLength.explanation: A string is a vector of characters, so `⍴` gives its length.

reverseString.title: Reverse a string
reverseString.explanation: `⌽` works on any vector, characters included.

indexOfLastOne.title: Position of the last 1
indexOfLastOne.prompt: Find the position of the last 1 inside 0 1 0 1 1 0 1 0.
indexOfLastOne.explanation: `⍸` returns the positions of all the `1`s: `2 4 5 7`. `⌽` reverses them to `7 5 4 2`. `⊃` takes the first one: that is the position of the last original `1`.

inverse2x2.title: Invert a 2×2 matrix
inverse2x2.prompt: Invert the matrix whose rows are 1 2 and 3 4.
inverse2x2.explanation: Monadic `⌹` computes the inverse of a square matrix. Determinant of `(1 2; 3 4)` is `1·4 - 2·3 = ¯2`, and the rest follows from the standard 2×2 formula.

hexDecode.title: Hexadecimal decoding
hexDecode.prompt: Decode the digits 1 0 0 read in base 16.
hexDecode.explanation: Digits `1 0 0` in base 16 are worth `1·256 + 0·16 + 0 = 256`. The familiar `0x100`.

iversonPrimes.title: Primality indicator
iversonPrimes.prompt: Produce a 0/1 indicator telling whether each number from 1 to 10 is prime.
iversonPrimes.explanation: Same construction as the primes-up-to-20 exercise, without the final filter. What remains is the mask (an Iverson vector): `1` where the number is prime, `0` elsewhere.

magnitudeSquared.title: Magnitude squared
magnitudeSquared.prompt: Compute the squared magnitude of the vector 3 4.
magnitudeSquared.explanation: `3 4 * 2` squares each element (`9 16`). `+/` sums to `25`. The squared magnitude of the vector.

l1Norm.title: L1 norm
l1Norm.explanation: Absolute values `3 1 4 1 5`, sum `14`. The taxicab distance from the origin.

tailOfVector.title: Tail of a vector
tailOfVector.prompt: Return every element of 10 20 30 40 except the first.

pascalSum.title: Sum of a row of Pascal's triangle
pascalSum.prompt: Compute the sum of row 8 of Pascal's triangle. The answer should equal 2 to the 8th power.
pascalSum.explanation: The sum of the binomial coefficients on row `n` of Pascal's triangle is always `2^n`. Here `n=8`.

indexOrigin.title: Index origin
indexOrigin.prompt: Show the index origin of the interpreter.
indexOrigin.explanation: `⎕IO` is the system variable that says where indexing starts. This interpreter uses `1`, the classic APL convention.

alphabetSystem.title: Uppercase alphabet
alphabetSystem.prompt: Print the uppercase alphabet using a system constant.
alphabetSystem.explanation: `⎕A` is a system constant containing the entire uppercase Latin alphabet as a 26-character vector.

digitsSystem.title: Digit string
digitsSystem.prompt: Print the ten decimal digits as a string.
digitsSystem.explanation: `⎕D` is the ten-digit string. Handy when mapping digit-characters into numeric values.

currentTimestamp.title: Current timestamp
currentTimestamp.prompt: Read the current timestamp as a vector.
currentTimestamp.result: vector of 7 numbers (year month day hour minute second millisecond)
currentTimestamp.explanation: `⎕TS` snapshots the current instant into seven integers.

year.title: Current year
year.prompt: Read only the current year from the timestamp.
year.explanation: `⊃⎕TS` takes the first element of the timestamp.

alphabetReversed.title: Alphabet reversed
alphabetReversed.prompt: Print the alphabet from Z down to A.

executeSimple.title: Execute text
executeSimple.prompt: Parse and run the string '1+2' as if it were typed.
executeSimple.explanation: `⍎` (execute) takes a string and evaluates it as if it were APL code typed by hand.

executeSum.title: Execute a reduction
executeSum.explanation: Same idea: the string is parsed as any APL line would be.

executeMatrix.title: Execute a matrix expression
executeMatrix.prompt: Execute the string '3 3 ⍴ ⍳9' to build a 3×3 matrix.
executeMatrix.explanation: The string carries the full matrix formula. `⍎` runs it as though you had typed it.

tradfnInc.title: Tradfn-style increment
tradfnInc.prompt: Define inc inside a ∇ … ∇ block and apply it to 9.
tradfnInc.explanation: The markers `∇ … ∇` delimit a traditional function definition. Here they wrap a small dfn that adds `1`.

tradfnSum.title: Tradfn alias for sum
tradfnSum.prompt: Bind tot to +/ using a ∇ block and apply it to ⍳100.
tradfnSum.explanation: `tot` gets aliased to `+/` inside `∇ … ∇`. Applied to `1..100` it returns Gauss's sum.

tradfnDistance.title: Tradfn for Euclidean distance
tradfnDistance.prompt: Inside a ∇ block, bind dist to the Euclidean distance and call it on 0 0 and 3 4.

letterIndex.title: Letter index inside the alphabet
letterIndex.prompt: Find the position of each letter of 'CAT' in the alphabet.
letterIndex.explanation: `⎕A ⍳ 'CAT'` looks up each letter inside the alphabet and reports its position: C → 3, A → 1, T → 20.

digitFromChar.title: From digit characters to integers
digitFromChar.prompt: Turn the characters of '42' into the integers 4 and 2.
digitFromChar.explanation: `⎕D ⍳ '42'` finds `'4'` and `'2'` among the digits, at positions 5 and 3 (1-based). `¯1+` shifts them back into the values `0..9`.

executeBuildExpr.title: Build and execute an expression
executeBuildExpr.prompt: Compose the string '×/' followed by '1 2 3 4 5' and execute it.
executeBuildExpr.explanation: Builds an APL string on the fly: `⍕⍳5` produces `'1 2 3 4 5'`, catenation prepends `'×/'`, and `⍎` runs the resulting source.

matchEqualVectors.title: Match two equal vectors
matchEqualVectors.prompt: Check whether 1 2 3 and 1 2 3 are structurally equal.
matchEqualVectors.explanation: `≡` returns 1 only when both arrays have the same shape and every element matches.

matchUnequalVectors.title: Match two different vectors
matchUnequalVectors.prompt: Check whether 1 2 3 and 1 2 4 are structurally equal.
matchUnequalVectors.explanation: Shapes match but one element differs, so `≡` returns 0.

matchShapeMismatch.title: Match with different shapes
matchShapeMismatch.prompt: Compare two vectors of different length.
matchShapeMismatch.explanation: When shapes differ `≡` returns 0 without inspecting individual values.

depthScalar.title: Depth of a scalar
depthScalar.prompt: Ask for the nesting depth of the scalar 5.
depthScalar.explanation: Monadic `≡` returns 0 for any simple scalar, since there is no nesting.

depthFlatVector.title: Depth of a flat vector
depthFlatVector.explanation: A simple, non-nested array has depth 1.

tallyVector.title: Tally a vector
tallyVector.explanation: Monadic `≢` returns the length of the first axis. For a vector that is its element count.

tallyMatrixRows.title: Tally the rows of a matrix
tallyMatrixRows.prompt: How many rows in a 3 by 4 matrix?
tallyMatrixRows.explanation: `≢` measures the first axis. For a matrix that is the row count.

notMatchVectors.title: Not match two vectors
notMatchVectors.prompt: Verify that 1 2 3 and 1 2 4 do not match.
notMatchVectors.explanation: `≢` is the logical negation of `≡`: 1 when the arrays differ in shape or values.

palindromeWithMatch.title: Palindrome check with match
palindromeWithMatch.prompt: Reverse 1 2 3 2 1 and check whether the result matches the original.
palindromeWithMatch.explanation: `⌽` reverses the vector. `≡` confirms the structural equality element by element.

tallyStringLength.title: String length via tally
tallyStringLength.explanation: On a character vector `≢` returns its length, just like `⍴` for a vector but always returning a scalar.

zildeShape.title: Shape of zilde
zildeShape.prompt: What is the shape of the empty numeric vector ⍬?
zildeShape.explanation: `⍬` is the canonical empty numeric vector, length 0, rank 1.

sumOfEmpty.title: Sum of zilde
sumOfEmpty.explanation: Reducing an empty vector returns the identity element of the function. For `+` that is 0.

buildVectorFromZilde.title: Catenate onto zilde
buildVectorFromZilde.explanation: Catenating to zilde is a no-op, useful when accumulating in a loop that starts empty.

partitionCount.title: Count partition groups
partitionCount.explanation: `⊆` starts a new group whenever the left value rises (or repeats a different positive value) and drops items where the left value is 0.

partitionFirstGroup.title: First partition group
partitionFirstGroup.prompt: Disclose the first partition of 'abcdefg' under 1 1 2 2 0 3 3.
partitionFirstGroup.explanation: `⊃` picks the first item of the partitioned result, which is the character vector 'ab'.

nestRaisesDepth.title: Nest raises depth
nestRaisesDepth.prompt: Wrap 1 2 3 with ⊆ and check its new depth.
nestRaisesDepth.explanation: `⊆` on a non-scalar wraps it into a scalar container, lifting the depth from 1 to 2.

powerIncrement.title: Apply increment ten times
powerIncrement.prompt: Start from 0 and add 1 ten times with ⍣.
powerIncrement.explanation: `f⍣N` applies `f` to its argument `N` times in sequence. Equivalent to a tight `for` loop with no body to write.

powerDoubling.title: Double a value eight times
powerDoubling.prompt: Start from 1 and double it eight times.
powerDoubling.explanation: Each iteration multiplies by 2. After 8 iterations the value is 2 to the 8th, that is 256.

rankSumRows.title: Sum each row with rank
rankSumRows.prompt: Sum every row of a 3 by 4 matrix of ⍳12.
rankSumRows.explanation: `+/⍤1` says: apply `+/` to every 1-cell (every row). The result is a vector of row sums.

atReplaceAtIndex.title: Replace at a single index
atReplaceAtIndex.explanation: `{0}@2` applies the constant function `{0}` at index 2 of the right argument.

atReplaceMany.title: Replace at multiple indices
atReplaceMany.explanation: When the right operand of `@` is a vector of indices, the function is applied at each of them.

quoteQuadShape.title: Shape of quote-quad
quoteQuadShape.explanation: In this interpreter `⍞` (character console I/O) reads as an empty character vector, since no terminal input is wired.

minReduce.title: Minimum via reduce
minReduce.prompt: Find the smallest value of 4 2 7 1 9 3 using a reduction.
minReduce.explanation: `⌊` between two numbers keeps the smaller one. `⌊/` folds that across the vector, leaving the global minimum.

rangeSpread.title: Range of a vector
rangeSpread.prompt: Compute the spread (max minus min) of 5 1 9 3 7.
rangeSpread.explanation: `⌈/` gives `9`, `⌊/` gives `1`. Subtracting them yields the range. A one-liner for statistical spread.

countNonZero.title: Count non-zero values
countNonZero.prompt: How many non-zero values are in 0 3 0 5 0 2?
countNonZero.explanation: `0 ≠ v` marks every position whose value differs from zero. `+/` sums those marks.

allEqualToFive.title: Check all equal to 5
allEqualToFive.prompt: Verify that every element of 5 5 5 5 equals 5.
allEqualToFive.explanation: `v = 5` produces a boolean mask. `∧/` is the AND of all bits, so it returns `1` only when every position matched.

anyTrue.title: At least one true
anyTrue.prompt: Is there any 1 in the vector 0 0 1 0 0?
anyTrue.explanation: `∨/` is the OR of all bits. As soon as a single `1` appears, the reduction is `1`.

firstDifference.title: First differences
firstDifference.explanation: `1↓v` drops the first element, `¯1↓v` drops the last. Subtracting them position-wise yields each gap between neighbours.

arrayMaxFlat.title: Maximum across a matrix
arrayMaxFlat.prompt: Find the largest value anywhere inside a 2×3 matrix of 1 5 2 9 3 4.
arrayMaxFlat.explanation: Comma flattens the matrix into a vector. `⌈/` then reduces that vector to its single largest value.

dropOnString.title: Drop characters
dropOnString.prompt: Drop the first two characters of 'apl'.
dropOnString.explanation: `↓` works on character vectors exactly as it does on numeric ones.

doubleNegation.title: Double negation
doubleNegation.explanation: Right to left: `-5` is `¯5`, then `-` again returns `5`. Two sign flips cancel.

rightFoldSubtract.title: Right-fold subtraction
rightFoldSubtract.prompt: Reduce 10 3 2 with subtraction and explain why the answer is not 5.
rightFoldSubtract.explanation: Reductions in APL fold from the right. `-/ 10 3 2` is `10 - (3 - 2)`, which equals `10 - 1 = 9`. A common surprise for newcomers.

encodeTimeOfDay.title: Seconds to h:m:s
encodeTimeOfDay.prompt: Convert 3725 seconds into hours, minutes and seconds.
encodeTimeOfDay.explanation: `⊤` encodes a number against a mixed base. With bases `24 60 60` you split `3725` into 1 hour, 2 minutes and 5 seconds.

floorDivision.title: Floor division
floorDivision.explanation: `17÷5` is `3.4`. Monadic `⌊` rounds toward minus infinity, yielding `3`.

distinctCharCount.title: Distinct letters in a word
distinctCharCount.prompt: How many distinct letters are there in 'mississippi'?
distinctCharCount.explanation: `∪` keeps one copy of every character: `m`, `i`, `s`, `p`. `⍴` reports the length of that vector.

matrixMaxByRow.title: Row-wise maximum
matrixMaxByRow.prompt: For a 2×3 matrix of 1 5 2 9 3 4, find the largest value in each row.
matrixMaxByRow.explanation: `⌈/` over a matrix reduces along the last axis: one maximum per row. Row 1 maxes at `5`, row 2 at `9`.

countMatching.title: Count matching values
countMatching.prompt: How many times does 7 appear in 7 3 7 1 7?
countMatching.explanation: `7 = v` produces a boolean mask of matches. `+/` sums to the count.

harmonicMean.title: Harmonic mean
harmonicMean.prompt: Compute the harmonic mean of 2 and 6.
harmonicMean.explanation: Harmonic mean: `n` divided by the sum of reciprocals. Here `2 ÷ (½+⅙) = 2 ÷ ⅔ = 3`. `÷v` produces the reciprocals, `+/` sums them, `(⍴v)÷` divides.

composeFunctions.title: Compose two functions
composeFunctions.prompt: Define f as +1, g as ×2, then apply f after g to 5.
composeFunctions.explanation: Right to left, as always: `g 5` is `10`, then `f 10` is `11`. Function composition in APL is just juxtaposition.

interleaveZeros.title: Interleave with zeros
interleaveZeros.prompt: Insert a zero after every value of 7 8 9 10.
interleaveZeros.explanation: Expand `\` uses the mask `1 0 1 0 1 0 1` as a stencil: every `1` consumes the next source value, every `0` inserts a fill (zero for numerics).

clampUpper.title: Cap values at five
clampUpper.explanation: Dyadic `⌊` returns the smaller of the two arguments. With a scalar left, each right-hand value is clipped down to that ceiling.

sumOfBoolean.title: Sum a boolean vector
sumOfBoolean.explanation: `+/` on a 0/1 vector is just a popcount: how many bits are set.
